channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Melkbosstrand Beach Closed for Third Time in Three Weeks Due to Separate Sewage Incidents
item.description: Melkbosstrand Beach, a popular West Coast spot near Cape Town, has faced repeated temporary closures over the past three weeks due to sewage-related problems, leaving local residents increasingly frus...

item.title: UNILAG Lecturers Begin Indefinite Strike Over Partial Payment of January and February 2026 Salaries
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) at the University of Lagos don declare say dem go enter indefinite strike from Wednesday, March 11, 2026, because their January and February salaries no...

item.title: Madagascar President reshuffles government after seizing power
item.description: Madagascar's President Michael Randrianirina, who came into power in October following youth-led protests that ousted his predecessor, has dismissed the country's prime minister and the entire cabinet...

item.title: Bahrain arrests six individuals, including five Pakistanis, over videos related to Iran attacks
item.description: Bahrain’s Interior Ministry has confirmed the arrest of six Asian nationals, including five Pakistanis and one Bangladeshi, by the Anti-Cyber Crime Directorate. Authorities allege that the suspects fi...

item.title: Stretch Out Strap: A Practical Tool to Improve Flexibility and Relieve Muscle Tension
item.description: Many people neglect stretching, which can lead to muscle tightness, back pain, and reduced mobility over time. Physical therapist Gavin Hamer emphasizes that stretching helps maintain healthy joints, ...

item.title: Porsche plans cost reductions and continued combustion-engine models to stabilise business
item.description: Porsche’s new CEO, Michael Leiters, is prioritising cost reduction and a continued focus on combustion-engine vehicles as part of a strategy to restore the struggling sportscar manufacturer. Since its...

item.title: SM Mall of Asia hosts 'The Only Begotten Son' art exhibit for reflection this Lent
item.description: This Lenten season, SM Mall of Asia invites the public to experience 'The Only Begotten Son,' an art exhibit at MOA Sky running from March 2 to April 12. The exhibit transforms the mall’s skyline spac...

item.title: Mirra Andreeva Loses Temper in Indian Wells Defeat to Siniakova, Smashes Racket and Shouts at Crowd
item.description: Teenage tennis prodigy Mirra Andreeva, the defending champion at Indian Wells and world No. 8, suffered a surprising third-round exit on March 9, 2026, losing to unseeded Czech player Katerina Siniako...

item.title: Fifteen Authors Longlisted for Carol Shields Prize for Fiction
item.description: The longlist for the Carol Shields Prize for Fiction, one of the world's most significant literary awards for English-language books by women and non-binary authors, has been announced. The US$150,000...

item.title: How to Distinguish High Commissions from Embassies
item.description: Many Kenyans get confused between embassies and high commissions, but there's a simple way to tell them apart. Embassies represent countries that are not part of the Commonwealth, like the United Stat...

item.title: Marcos pitches Philippines' bid for UN Security Council non-permanent seat, calls for principled peace amid global conflicts
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. personally appealed for international support during a special plenary session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York on March 10, 2026, as the Philippines s...

item.title: Cheltenham Festival 2026: Key Horses and Results from Champions Day
item.description: The 2026 Cheltenham Festival opened with Champions Day showcasing top-tier National Hunt racing. Fans witnessed an action-packed start as horses such as Kopek Des Bordes, Lossiemouth, and The New Lion...

item.title: WSJ Live Q&A on Stock Markets, Iran Oil Disruptions, and Economic Impacts
item.description: The Wall Street Journal hosted a live Q&A session on March 10, 2026, amid volatile stock market conditions driven by escalating tensions in the Iran conflict. Panelists addressed investor concerns abo...

item.title: Debating Controversial Topics in Schools Sparks Discussion on Free Speech and Cultural Safety
item.description: In 2025, Sonja Lowen, chairperson of Debating South Australia, set a Year 9 debate topic on the 'trad-wife movement', asking students to discuss whether it benefits women. The debate drew swift backla...

item.title: BMNVC initiative launched in Udhagamandalam to address near vision problems
item.description: In Udhagamandalam, The Nilgiris, the BMNVC (Bharat Mission for Near Vision Care) was officially launched on March 10, 2026. This programme is aimed at restoring near vision, particularly addressing pr...

item.title: Newsroom Exposure to Violent Content Is Impacting Journalists’ Mental Health
item.description: Recent research highlights the growing psychological toll on journalists working in newsrooms exposed to constant violent or distressing content. While traditional safety protocols have long protected...

item.title: PCCB recovers funds and vehicles from mismanaged development projects in Singida
item.description: In Singida Region, the Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) successfully recovered 119.16 million shillings and four vehicles following investigations into 25 development projects wort...

item.title: Nationals prepare for leadership ballot following Littleproud’s resignation
item.description: David Littleproud has unexpectedly stepped down as leader of the Nationals, catching both his party and Coalition colleagues off guard. The resignation was announced after a courtesy call to Prime Min...

item.title: CBI schedules further questioning of TVK chief Vijay in Karur stampede case
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has issued a fresh summons to actor and Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president, Vijay, for further questioning in connection with the Karur stampede case, n...

item.title: Motorists Ask NTSA For Details On Instant Traffic Fines Process
item.description: A Kenyan motorists' lobby, the Motorists Association of Kenya (MAK), is pushing the National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) to provide more clarity on the newly launched instant traffic fines s...
